programmes.show_description: 1960. Drama. Directed by Edmond T. Greville and starring Noelle Adam, Christopher Lee and Oliver Reed. Paul, marries a French lady but his daughter is determined to spoil the relationship.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Once Upon a Time
programmes.show_description: 1967. A wonderful trip to Denmark to look at the Danish way of life.
1959. Crime Drama. Director: William Martin. Stars Jeanne Rainer, Ronald Long & Art Koulias. A young model is kidnapped and threatened into working for a gang. Will she be able to escape?
A Place of Still Waters. Western with Wayne Rogers & Robert Bray, who run a stagecoach line in the Old West where they come across a wide variety of killers, robbers and ladies in distress.
Charcoal Burners/Pigeon Shoot. 1986. Countryman Jack Hargreaves follows the men who produce charcoal and narrates an archive film of one of the largest pigeon flocks he had ever seen.
1956. Western. Directed by Albert C. Gannaway & Ismael Rodríguez. Stars Bruce Bennett & Lon Chaney Jr. Frontiersman Daniel Boone and his family fight for survival when his settlement is attacked.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Keeping Tabs on Space
programmes.show_description: 1967. A fascinating look at Britain's first spacecraft - UK3, with a glimpse at how it was created, tested and launched.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. Dean and Thabo complete their mission only to be thrown into another dangerous quest straight away!
Sir Crustabread. 1957. Stars William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. King Arthur gives Sir Lancelot a new name and sends him away to help a lady being forced into marriage.
The Sport of Penguins. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward, Yvonne Craig & Ethel Merman. The Penguin teams up with Lola Lasagne to switch racehorses to make money by betting on the real Parasol.
Three Masked Men. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger and Dale Evans. Outlaws try to kidnap the governor during Mineral City's anniversary celebration but Roy thwarts the plot.
The Trap. 1967. Stars Sylvia Anderson, Paul Maxwell & Francis Matthews. Captain Scarlet gets dispatched to supervise security at the International Air Conference after the Mysterons latest threat.
Manhunt. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Trapped on an island owned by Prince Erik, a man who enjoys hunting prey, Tell's careers appears to be at the end.
Dead Man's Rock. 1957. Stars Robert Shaw, Richard Pasco & Edwin Richfield. Spaniards attack while the Sultana is beached, and Governor Beamish is kidnapped. Tempest must find a way to rescue him.
The Prisoner. 1956. Starring: Bernadette O'Farrell, Donald Pleasence & Richard Greene. A rumour spreads that Richard is dead and John acts quickly to claim the throne.
1960. Sci-fi. Directed by David Bradley. Stars Ken Clark, Michi Kobi & Tom Conway. An international team embarks on an expedition to the moon where they encounter a dangerous alien intelligence.
1957. Action. Directed by William Fairchild. Stars Laurence Harvey, Dawn Addams & Michael Craig. During WWII, the British must prevent the Italian Navy from attaching mines to their warships.
A nostalgic look back at British soul band Hot Chocolate as they perform the 1974 song Emma.
Sarong. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Anthony Wickert & Joe McCormick. Cobb escorts a group of women on their way to take up servant duties but he is concerned about the honesty of the man organizing.
1960. Comedy. Director: Carol Reed. Stars Alec Guinness, Maureen O'Hara & Burl Ives. Vacuum cleaner salesman Jim agrees to work as an agent and recruit agents for the British Secret Service.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life On the Scent
programmes.show_description: 1967. A magnificent glimpse into the perfume and fragrance industry, with a look at how perfumes and scents are being used more and more in everyday life.
The Lost Life. 1963. Stars Rupert Davies, Henry Oscar & Olga Lindo. To Inspector Lognon the life lost was just another murder to be solved, but it meant more than that for Maigret.
The Bargain. 1976. Stars Gemma Jones, Christopher Cazenove & John Cater. Heavily in debt, Louisa is on a juggernaut that lands in her in the hospital, after she collapses from exhaustion.
1972. Drama. Director: Brian G. Hutton. Stars Elizabeth Taylor, Micheal Caine & Susannah York. The venomous wife of a wealthy architect tries, at length, to break up her husband and his mistress.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life In Good Shape
programmes.show_description: 1962. A focus on modern design in Britain, with a look at some of the recent exhibits at the Design Centre.
The Bride Possessed. 1959. Stars Virginia Leith, Skip Homeier & Harry Townes. Matt Conroy is startled when on their honeymoon his wife Sally suddenly is a determined woman, who denies knowing him.
1995. Hosted by Antoine de Caunes and Jean Paul Gaultier. EuroTrash takes Japan to explore the Toto Toilet factory, where toilets are scented and heated and meet Japan's favourite weather woman.
2025. Director: Marc Ball. Reflect on how Bruce Lee revolutionised Hollywood, an industry dominated by racial stereotypes. His legacy has redefined roles and paves a new way for Asian actors.
1968. Horror. Directors: Gerardo de Leon & Eddie Romero. Stars John Ashley, Kent Taylor & Mario Montenegro. Tropical island natives must contend with man eating plants and animals!

The Beginning Part 2. 1973. Stars Keir Dullea, Gay Rowan & William Osler. The farmers travel the ship to various cultures in search for someone who can correct the course of the ship and save mankind.
The Plunge of Peril. 1941. Starring: Warren Hull. Brett returns & wants to get to the bottom of the racket. A helpful lead suggests that the crime syndicate has its fingers in more than one pie.
The Magic Book. 1956. Stars William Russell, Ronald Leigh-Hunt & Cyril Smith. Sir Lancelot helps to keep safe an Abbey and the occupants from invaders.
1965. The Princess and The Paupers. Classic crime series produced by Aaron Spelling and starring Anne Francis. A pop singer is being held captive for ransom money, but is all as it seems?
1947. Comedy. Directed by Elliott Nugent. Stars Bob Hope, Dorothy Lamour & Peter Lorre. A photographer on death row recounts the events that led to his conviction to a group of reporters.
1947. Action. Director: John Rawlins. Stars Boris Karloff, Ralph Byrd & Anne Gwynne. Dick Tracy goes up against a villain who robs banks using a nerve gas.
1953. Western. Director: Ray Nazarro. Stars Sterling Hayden, Eve Miller, Barton MacLane. An army engineer is sent to oversee the construction of the Kansas Pacific railroad and stop any saboteurs.
1939. Mystery. Director: Thorold Dickinson. Stars Leslie Banks, Greta Gynt, Ian McLean. Poison, death and mystery set at the Arsenal Stadium. Detective Slade is called to solve the crime.
A Bride for Jed. 1964. Stars Buddy Ebsen, Lester Flatt & Joi Lansing. Mr. and Mrs. Drysdale try to help Jed find a wife and they come up with a unique idea with the help of some country musicians.
Ice Spy. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Alan Napier. Mr. Freeze is back in town and he plans to build an icy weapon that will have the world at his control.
1955. Drama. Director: Edward Dmytryk. Stars Deborah Kerr, Van Johnson & John Mills. In WW2 London, a writer falls in love with the wife of a civil servant. Both men suspect her of infidelity.
Groove along to British pop group The Avons and their song Do It With You (I Wanna Do It).
1985. The classic game show Give us a Clue hosted by Michael Parkinson, this week's guests are Jim Bowen, Anna Dawson, Paul Jones, Julia McKenzie, Kenneth Williams & Wincey Willis.
The Fiddlesticks Affair. 1965. Stars Robert Vaughn, David McCallum & Leo G. Carroll. Solo and Kuryakin plan to blow up a Thrush treasury containing $55 million. The facility's cover is a casino.
Travel to 1964 and take a trip from Oz to London, then to The Rover Factory in Coventry in the 1930s and Mike and Noel have found a brilliant lost reel of The Dave Clark Five.
The Good Samaritan. 1989. Stars Robert Powell, Sharon Maughan & Marc Zuber. The pleasures of travelling on the Trans Europe Express from Venice are often relished! But not for Hannay...
A Brush with the Law. 1983. Stars Mollie Sugden, Christopher Blake & Jennifer Lonsdale. Ida accidentally steals a blouse. She has to attend court where she makes an unexpected friend!
Cross Channel. 1955. Stars Ron Randell, Patrick Holt & Paula Byrne. Ellen and Charles meet in Paris where they plan to kill her husband. Things take a dark turn when Ellen learns she is in danger.
The Case of the Killer's Mark. 1964. Stars John Barrie, William Gaunt & Charles Morgan. A courting couple find the body of a woman in a derelict beach house, her forehead marked with the letter S.
1972. With Freddie Starr and Peter Goodwright, guest acts such as Arthur Mullard, Lance Percival and Johnny More play a number of roles in a series of sketches and songs!
1958. Crime. Directed by Don Siegel. Stars Eli Wallach, Robert Keith & Richard Jaeckel. A ruthless hitman is sent to recover packages of drugs hidden inside the luggage of unsuspecting travellers.

programmes.show_name: The Heritage Chart Show with Mike Read
programmes.show_description: A TOTP style TV Show for Heritage Artists based on the Heritage Chart which is voted on in over 80 countries. Songs you love from popular artists, live and on video with the odd interview.
